mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent indicates deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 55 percent of cases when both series rise concurrently over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, yielding positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive returns during recovery phases, with a regression t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter confirms robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Returns are negative in more than 60 percent of quarters where GDP growth surpasses the 2.0 percent threshold over a 15-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stable or increasing federal funds rates signals real rate tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns in more than 58 percent of observations during tightening periods across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect consistent job creation and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive weekly returns, with a Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a late-cycle expansion indicate peak consumer exhaustion and impending demand contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines do not precede negative returns in late-cycle phases, with predictive accuracy below 45 percent over a 20-year sample.
